name: ngn/rarity-ranking
title: Rarity Ranking
description: Ranked list with accent colors, images, and click-to-expand details.

attributes.headline: Ranked by rarity
attributes.subheadline: From the merely uncommon to the genuinely once-in-a-lifetime.

subheadline.default: From the merely uncommon to the genuinely once-in-a-lifetime.

default.name: Joseph
default.imageAlt: Joseph the cotton candy clawed lobster
default.expandedNote: Named after the biblical figure with a 'coat of many colors,' Joseph waves orange, blue, purple, green, and pink claws when he emerges from his rock cave.

default.name: Jackie
default.expandedNote: Short for Jack O'Lantern, named for her orange and black coloration and her arrival near Halloween. The veteran lobster captain typically brings rare catches home in his lunchbox.

default.name: Neptune
default.expandedNote: The first rare lobster to arrive at the Marine Science Center, Neptune's brilliant cobalt color comes from a disrupted binding process between the blue pigment crustacyanin and the red pigment astaxanthin.

default.name: Common Brown
default.expandedNote: The greenish-brown coloring of typical American lobsters serves as camouflage on the ocean floor. Their range extends from Cape Hatteras, North Carolina to Labrador, Canada — with New England having the highest concentration.
